title: ACIR Document v0.3.2
description: Formal JSON-schema for ACIR v0.3 documents (supports both 0.3.0 and 0.3.1). v0.3.1 adds the string form for $calculate (named reference to module.transforms[]). Everything else — data primitives, F_FOREACH, F_GUARD, $auth, $context, $verify, $validate, $conditional — is unchanged between 0.3.0 and 0.3.1. Semantic checks (e.g. $auth in public route, $calculate DSL parse, $input wildcard scope, output-type-matches-body) are enforced by the validator beyond what JSON-schema can express.

acir_kind.description: Document kind. Absent ⇒ "module" (fully backward-compatible). "project" ⇒ multi-component manifest (carries `project`, not `module`).

System.description: Roadmap v0.3+ multi-module support â€” out of scope for v0.3.0 (single-module). Accepted shape-wise to leave the door open.

Unit.description: X_UNIT — business logic composition. The `output` shape and unit body's effective return are cross-checked at validator level (output: scalar on IO_QUERY without limit:1/single:true is rejected).

Operation.description: All flow-control + I/O primitives. Each entry is dispatched by its `primitive` field.

FNative.description: Typed escape hatch. The validator validates the BOUNDARY (input/output TypeRef, declared effects), NEVER the `impl` body. Allowed as a unit `body` or as a step op of F_SEQUENCE (via Operation) — never as a DataExpr (absent from DataExpr.oneOf). The compiler injects the target block verbatim.

FBranch.description: Runtime branch â€” `condition` is a Predicate (boolean expression). v0.2 had a degenerate form without runtime conditions ; v0.3 requires the predicate.

FForeach.description: Iterate over a list. `mode: sequential` (default) await each iter ; `mode: parallel` issue all in parallel (Promise.all/CompletableFuture.allOf/asyncio.gather). `on_error: halt` is the only valid value in v0.3.

as.description: Loop variable name accessible via {$loop: "<name>.<field>"} inside `do`. Default: "item".

FilterExpr.description: Canonical filter (object map): each key is a field name and the value is either {operator, value} (canonical), a bare DataExpr (shorthand for eq), or `when_present` for conditional filters. Multi-key filters AND the predicates together.

DataObject.description: Object whose values are DataExprs. Used in IO_MUTATE.data, $transform.input, $verify, etc.

DataExpr.description: Anything that produces a value. Either a literal (string/number/bool/null), a $-ref, or a derived expression. The `not` clauses below explicitly forbid `$param` (hard-deprecated synonym of $input, decision Q1).

InputRef.description: Reference to the unit's input. Bare `$input` (empty string) refers to the whole input when it's a D_SCALAR. Wildcard `*` only allowed inside $calculate.formula and as F_FOREACH.items source (enforced at validator level, not in pure JSON-schema).

AuthRef.description: Read a JWT claim. Standard claims recognized: user_id/id/sub, email/username/upn, role/roles/groups. Custom claims accessed by exact name.

ContextRef.description: Read from the request scope (non-auth). Must use a namespace prefix: `request.*` (HTTP context like ip, user_agent) or `tenant.*` (multi-tenant middleware).

StepRef.description: Reference to a prior step result inside an F_SEQUENCE. Format: `<step_name>` or `<step_name>.<field>` (dot-access on the step's output entity).

LoopRef.description: Reference to the F_FOREACH loop variable. Format: `<loop_var>.<field>` (dot-access).

GenerateExpr.description: Generate a value at compile/runtime. String form for builtins (uuid/now/sequence/ulid/nanoid). Dict form for parameterized generators (slug, template).

format.description: template only: format string with placeholders

CalculateExpr.description: Computed field via a strict DSL (arithmetic + named aggregators + dot-access + wildcard inside aggregators only). Two forms supported : (1) inline dict with `{formula, inputs}` â€” the formula is parsed by the validator's BNF grammar ; (2) v0.3.1 string form `$calculate: "<transform_name>"` â€” named reference resolved against module.transforms[]. The referenced transform's body must itself be a `$calculate` dict so the formula can be inlined at the call site.

oneOf.description: v0.3.1 â€” named reference to module.transforms[<this-name>]. The transform's body must be `{$calculate: {formula, ...}}` ; the formula is inlined at compile time.

TransformExpr.description: Call a named transform declared in module.transforms. Body must be PURE â€” no IO_QUERY/IO_MUTATE inside (validator-enforced, decision Q2).

VerifyExpr.description: Verify a plaintext value against its stored hash. Compiled to AuthService.verifyPassword() in the target.

ValidateExpr.description: Call a named validator declared in module.validators. Returns a boolean ; typically used in F_GUARD.condition.

ConditionalExpr.description: Ternary expression. `if` is a Predicate, `then`/`else` are DataExprs.

HashExpr.description: Hash a plaintext value (typically a password before persist). Compiled to AuthService.hashPassword().

MergeExpr.description: Patch/merge multiple DataExprs into a single object literal. Used in IO_MUTATE.data for partial updates.

Predicate.description: Boolean expression. Used in F_BRANCH.condition, F_GUARD.condition, $conditional.if.

Contract.description: Field-level (or record-level) constraint. Compiled to Zod/Hibernate-Validator/Pydantic at the HTTP boundary (C_* stays boundary-only; cross-field server-side checks use module.validators[]).

TransformDecl.description: Top-level pure transform declaration. Body is restricted to data primitives + flow primitives (F_FOREACH/F_BRANCH) WITHOUT any IO_*. Validator enforces purity recursively (decision Q2).

body.description: Pure value expression ($calculate canonical) OR pure flow (F_FOREACH/F_BRANCH with value-producing branches). Accept-any-object here on purpose: a transform body is a pure DSL whose real contract (no IO_*) is enforced semantically by the validator (TRANSFORM_IMPURITY), not by JSON-schema. A strict Operation shape wrongly rejects canonical value-flow transforms (e.g. F_BRANCH then:{$loop}/{$merge}) — cf. the ecommerce-v0.3 example.

ValidatorDecl.description: Top-level server-side validator declaration. Returns a boolean ; called via {$validate} inside F_GUARD.condition. NOT emitted as Zod/Hibernate (decision Q5: server-side only).

RelationDecl.description: Top-level first-class relation. `on_delete` is intentionally absent (decision Q4) â€” each compiler applies its framework default.

ProjectComponent.description: One component = one microservice. References (or inlines) a single-`module` ACIR doc, plus its target/runtime/dependencies.

$component.description: path/key of the component's ACIR doc

runtime.description: Runtime shape consumed by the infrastructure compiler (port/base_image/build/health/env). Permissive in 0.3.2 (like System) — to be hardened later.

ProjectManifest.description: Multi-component project manifest. First-class evolution of the `system` block (which becomes the degenerate 1-component case). `infrastructure` stays permissive (consumed as-is by the infrastructure compiler); the `db_isolation` knob (per_component|shared) lives on the service side.

IOCall.description: Synchronous HTTP call to ANOTHER component of the project (microservice). `module` must appear in the calling component's dependencies; the base URL is resolved at runtime from the `<MODULE>_URL` env injected by the infrastructure compiler. ACIR 0.3.2.

endpoint.description: Route of the target component, {x} placeholders resolved via path_params
